Unprotected Coverage within Urban Footpr:
WHY THIS LAYER EXISTS 
 Primary output of Step 5 of the Mission Beach Mapping Methodology (February 2026). Represents the 771.7 ha (79.3%) of the Urban Footprint that falls outside existing MNES and MSES statutory protections — the area where CCRC local planning controls are most critical. Derived by subtracting the MNES+MSES mask from the Urban Footprint using a geometric difference operation. 
 NOTE: This is a candidate layer, not the final planning MLES. It must be intersected with the MLES Foundation Layer (Step 2) to filter down to only ecologically significant land. That intersection produces the true Planning MLES. 
 ROLE IN METHODOLOGY: Step 5 output — identifies the jurisdictional planning gap requiring CCRC action. 
 
 ———————————————————————————————————————————————————————————— 
 
 WHAT THIS LAYER IS 
 
 The Planning MLES Candidate represents the portion of the Urban Footprint that falls outside existing MNES (Matters of National Environmental Significance) and MSES (Matters of State Environmental Significance) statutory protections. This is the area where CCRC local planning controls are most critical — habitat that has local environmental significance but is not already covered by federal or state frameworks. 
 
 HOW IT WAS DERIVED 
 
 Step 1: Reprojected Draft SRA (2026) from EPSG:4326 to EPSG:7855 
 
 Step 2: Merged 5 MNES statutory layers into a single dissolved polygon 
 
 Step 3: Merged 6 MSES statutory layers into a single dissolved polygon 
 
 Step 4: Combined MNES + MSES dissolved polygons into a single coverage mask 
 
 Step 5: Clipped the combined mask to the Urban Footprint extent 
 
 Step 6: Computed the geometric difference: Urban Footprint minus MNES+MSES mask 
 
 Result: The remaining area requiring local planning protection 
 
 MNES INPUTS (5 layers) 
 
 - Wet Tropics World Heritage Area 
 
 - Lowland Tropical Rainforest TEC 
 
 - Littoral Rainforest 
 
 - Littoral Rainforest Potential 
 
 - Broad leaf tea-tree woodlands 
 
 MSES INPUTS (6 layers) 
 
 - Protected Area - Nature Refuges 
 
 - Protected area - Estates 
 
 - Fish habitat area 
 
 - Marine park - highly protected zones 
 
 - Strategic Rehabilitation Area (2009) 
 
 - Draft Strategic Rehabilitation Area (2026) 
 
 LAYERS INTENTIONALLY EXCLUDED 
 
 The following layers exist in the MNES/MSES groups but were NOT included in the mask because they are habitat value inputs (used to build the MLES), not statutory protection designations: 
 
 - EPBC Cassowary Distribution and Corridors (Step 2 inputs) 
 
 - Regulated Vegetation layers (Step 2 inputs — the foundation of MLES itself) 
 
 - Wildlife habitat layers, Flora Survey Trigger Map (assessment layers) 
 
 - QLUMP (land use mapping only) 
 
 - Groundwater management areas (water resource management) 
 
 AREA STATISTICS 
 
 Urban Footprint total: 973.1 ha 
 
 MNES+MSES within Urban Footprint: 201.4 ha (20.7%) 
 
 Planning MLES Candidate: 771.7 ha (79.3% of Urban Footprint) 
 
 IMPORTANT NOTES 
 
 - This layer represents the CANDIDATE planning MLES. The full MLES (Step 2 of the methodology) has not yet been built. Once the Step 2 union layer is complete, it should be intersected with this candidate to produce the final planning MLES. 
 
 - The Draft SRA (2026) boundaries may change before finalisation. Re-run this workflow when the SRA is confirmed. 
 
 - Geometry fixes were applied to the Draft SRA layer during processing. 
 
 CRS: EPSG:7855 (GDA2020 MGA Zone 55) 
 
 Created: February 2026 
 
 Methodology reference: Mission Beach Mapping Methodology, Draft Round 3, December 2025, Step 5
Protected Coverage within Urban Footprin:
WHY THIS LAYER EXISTS
Intermediate output of the Step 5 Planning MLES workflow (February 2026). The MNES+MSES Combined Mask clipped to the Urban Footprint boundary — representing the 201.4 ha (20.7%) of the Urban Footprint that already has statutory federal or state environmental protection. Retained to clearly show what IS already protected within CCRC's planning jurisdiction, as a complement to the Planning MLES Candidate which shows what is NOT.
ROLE IN METHODOLOGY: Step 5 intermediate — the protected portion of the Urban Footprint.
MNES + MSES Combined Mask:
WHY THIS LAYER EXISTS
Intermediate output of the Step 5 Planning MLES workflow (February 2026). The union of the MNES Combined Mask and MSES Combined Mask — representing the total spatial extent of all statutory federal and state environmental protections across the project area. Used as the overlay layer in the final difference operation against the Urban Footprint.
ROLE IN METHODOLOGY: Step 5 intermediate — complete statutory protection coverage mask.
MSES Combined Mask:
WHY THIS LAYER EXISTS 
 
 Intermediate output of the Step 5 Planning MLES workflow (February 2026). Created by merging and dissolving 6 MSES statutory layers (Nature Refuges, Protected Estates, Fish Habitat Area, Marine Park Highly Protected Zones, SRA 2009, Draft SRA 2026) into a single polygon representing the full spatial extent of state environmental protections. Geometry fixes were applied before merging. Retained as a standalone layer for audit, verification, and re-running the workflow independently. 
 
 ROLE IN METHODOLOGY: Step 5 intermediate — one half of the MNES+MSES subtraction mask.
MNES Combined Mask:
WHY THIS LAYER EXISTS
Intermediate output of the Step 5 Planning MLES workflow (February 2026). Created by merging and dissolving 5 MNES statutory layers (Wet Tropics World Heritage Area, Lowland Tropical Rainforest TEC, Littoral Rainforest, Littoral Rainforest Potential, Broad leaf tea-tree woodlands) into a single polygon representing the full spatial extent of federal environmental protections. Retained as a standalone layer for audit, verification, and re-running the workflow independently.
ROLE IN METHODOLOGY: Step 5 intermediate — one half of the MNES+MSES subtraction mask.
